tected as early as day five days p.i. and peaked at 28 days p.i. at a ratio of 1:75.39 (Fig. 5).
Protection against lung lesions in gerbils by passive immunization
Passive immunization was used to evaluate the effects of humoral immunity in protecting
against EV71 lethal challenge. Gerbils were passively immunized with neutralizing antibodies
(GMT = 89; 100 μL/gerbil). Two doses fully protected gerbils from EV71 lethal challenge. In
Fig 4. Virus replication in lung and other tissues of EV71-infected gerbils. (A) gerbils (7, 14, 21, 28, 35 d old) were inoculated IP with 1×105.5 TCID50 of
EV71 (n = 6 for each age groups). (B) Virus titer in various tissues was determined in 21-day-old gerbils inoculated with 1×105.5 TCID50 of EV71 via IP route
(n = 8 for each dose group). (C) 21-day-old gerbils were inoculated with 1×103.5 or 1×105.5 TCID50 of EV71 via IP.
